lede: The full warehouse lifecycle end to end — model the site, receive goods, let reorder rules replenish, batch picks into waves, count stock, and publish a standard cost. Six screens, one continuous flow.

steps.title: Set up your warehouse structure
steps.narration: Warehouse Management is the physical layer — warehouses, then Locations and UoM Groups under Setup. Head Office already has its Default Warehouse, marked Active; add more to model extra sites.

steps.title: Receive goods with a procurement order
steps.narration: Under Procurement, click New Procurement Order to bring goods in from a supplier. Receiving against it posts the units into stock at the head-office warehouse.

steps.title: Auto-replenish from reorder rules
steps.narration: Replenishment turns min-max rules into purchase orders. Use Dry Run to preview, then Run & Generate POs to raise them automatically — or New Rule to define a fresh reorder point.

steps.title: Batch picks into a wave
steps.narration: Pick Waves group outbound move-lines into one picking run — planned, released, in progress, completed. Click New Wave and select the move lines from the inventory queue to release them together.

steps.title: Count stock with an audit session
steps.narration: Stock Audit verifies physical inventory. Start a Spot Check for a few SKUs, a Cycle Count for a rolling subset, or a Full Count for everything — posted variances adjust on-hand automatically.

steps.title: Publish a standard cost
steps.narration: Standard Costs set the valuation baseline. Publish Cost fixes a SKU's standard; Compute Variance compares it to actual purchase price so purchase-price variance flows to the ledger.
